states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection state before any command

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated; waiting for a transfer command

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for RNTO

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Commands including LIST, RETR, STOR, DELE, and RNFR must only be sent after a successful PASS response (230).
A data connection establishment command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should precede file transfer or listing commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command may be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
If authentication fails (e.g., 530 response), the client may restart the sequence with a new USER command.
The RNTO command must follow an RNFR command to complete a file renaming operation.
USER.username: Must contain a username string
PASS.password: Must contain a password string
RETR.filename: Must specify a file name or path
STOR.filename: Must specify a file name or path
RNFR.old_path: Must specify the source path to rename
RNTO.new_path: Must specify the destination path to rename

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, CAPI, PRUE, SMNT

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.
